meta.description: Human-readable game mechanics reference for World of Sea Battle

speedSystem.overview: Ships have 3 forward speed modes plus reverse. All speeds shown in displayed knots.

stop.description: No movement. Ship can still turn with axis controls.

combat.description: Second speed. More sails deployed. Standard combat speed.

full.description: Third speed / full sails. All sails deployed. Enables cruise speed ramp-up.

speedFormula.description: Final Speed = Base Speed × (1 + speed % bonuses) + flat speed bonuses
speedFormula.example: A ship with 4.5 knot base speed and a +10% bonus moves at ~5.0 knots

speedSystem.minimumSpeedNote: Speed never drops below 1.5 knots, even with heavy penalties

cruiseSpeed.overview: When at full sails (speed mode 3), going straight, and at max speed, ships gain bonus 'cruise' speed over time.

maxCruiseSpeed.formula: Cruise bonus = 3.5 × (1 + sail cruise bonus) × speed penalty factor
details: Faster ships get LESS cruise bonus — the system penalizes high base speed
A ship at 7.5 knots gets about 3.5 knots cruise bonus
A ship at 4.5 knots gets about 3.9 knots cruise bonus
Ships at 32.5+ knots get zero cruise bonus
maxCruiseSpeed.sailBonus: Certain sails (e.g. Triangular Sails) give +20% cruise speed

windBonus.description: Additional cruise speed bonus when sailing with favorable wind. Requires near-perfect wind alignment (heading ~35° off the wind direction).
windBonus.skillBonus: Base bonus of 2, plus any captain wind speed skills
windBonus.balloonException: Balloons require nearly perfect downwind alignment (within ~5°) for this bonus

cruiseSpeed.totalMaxCruise: Total cruise speed = base cruise bonus + wind cruise bonus

rampUp.description: Cruise speed builds up gradually over time while conditions are met
conditions: Must be at full sails (speed mode 3)
Must be at or near max speed
rampUp.rampRate: Faster ships ramp up cruise speed more quickly. Sail upgrades and speed-change bonuses increase ramp rate.
rampUp.lowHpPenalty: Ships below 25% HP get up to 33% slower cruise ramp-up
rampUp.minimumCruise: When at full sails, cruise speed is always at least 1.25 knots

whenNotFullSails.description: Cruise speed drops at about 7.4 per second when not at full sails

whenTurning.description: Turning while at full sails causes cruise speed loss. Higher mobility = MORE loss (sharper turns bleed more speed).
whenTurning.pilotCrewBonus: Pilot crew reduces turning cruise loss by 25%

whenDamaged.description: Taking hull or sail damage directly reduces accumulated cruise speed

displayFactor.description: The displayed max cruise accounts for sail damage and cargo weight penalties

holdCapacity.overview: Cargo weight affects ship speed through a capacity speed factor.

formula.description: Speed multiplier based on cargo load percentage

under50percent.description: BONUS speed when under 50% loaded. Lighter = faster.

from50to100percent.description: Linear speed penalty from 50% to 100% load.

over100percent.description: Cubic penalty for overweight — gets very harsh very quickly.

formula.raftException: The raft/rowboat is never affected by cargo weight.

sailSpeedFactor.description: Sail damage reduces speed proportionally. Speed factor is based on the weighted average health of all sail sections.

mobilityPenalty.description: Below 95% sail health, turning ability drops. At 80% sail health, mobility is only 80% of normal.

windEffectivity.description: How much wind helps or hinders your ship, based on your heading relative to wind direction.
windEffectivity.range: 0 (sailing directly into wind) to 1 (sailing with wind)
windEffectivity.balloonException: Balloons only benefit from direct downwind sailing

windCourseChange.description: Wind pushes your heading off course. The effect is stronger when sailing against the wind. Disabled by the Sailing Master crew member.
windCourseChange.paddleShipsExempt: Ships with paddles or steam wheels are not affected by strong wind turning

backwindPenalty.description: When sailing against the wind, speed is reduced by up to a third of your base speed

stormBonus.description: Storms add bonus speed from wind effects and from being near map edges

windEffects.riverEffect: Rivers provide minimum 50% wind effectiveness even when sailing against wind

uniqueWindRose.description: Ships with unique wind roses use a modified wind calculation. At nearly perfect downwind angles, effectiveness actually drops slightly.

paddlesAndSteam.overview: Ships with paddles or steam wheels can supplement sail speed.

paddleSpeed.description: Paddles only kick in when wind-driven speed is insufficient. Their output is limited by crew efficiency and cargo weight.
paddleSpeed.slowSpeedPenalty: At slow speed (mode 1), paddles operate at only 70% power

steamWheel.description: Steam wheels work like paddles but require coal. Can be toggled on/off. Uses coal on a 1-second timer when active.

cannonSpeedFactor.overview: Each cannon type has a projectile speed multiplier that affects how fast cannonballs travel.

values.0.8×: Slow projectiles: Cast Iron Howitzer, Iron Howitzer types, Heavy Howitzer, Swivel Gun
values.1.0×: Standard: Most base cannons, Bronze types, Mortars
values.1.25×: Fast projectiles: Long Barrel types, Culverin types, Demi-Culverin

cannonSpeedFactor.note: This multiplies the base projectile speed, stacking with cannonball speed bonuses from crew and upgrades.

damageFormula.description: Damage is applied through multiple components
damageFormula.healthDamage: Direct HP damage, reduced by damage reduction bonuses (capped at 99%)
damageFormula.sailDamage: Distributed across all sails or targeted at a specific sail
damageFormula.damageReduction: Damage reduction from upgrades and crew, capped at 99%
damageFormula.educationMapBonus: Players take only 67% damage in tutorial maps. PvP damage in tutorials is 1.2× normal.

cannonDamage.ballDamageBonus: Total cannon damage bonus = crew/upgrade damage bonuses + low-HP damage bonus (if applicable)
cannonDamage.lowHpDamageBonus: A bonus damage buff activates when your ship drops below 50% HP
cannonDamage.buildDamage: Bonus damage against buildings and fortifications
cannonDamage.frontBackBonus: Bow and stern cannons deal bonus damage from upgrades
cannonDamage.penetration: Weapon penetration bonus from flat armor-piercing upgrades
cannonDamage.cannonBallSpeed: Cannonball speed multiplier = 1 + speed bonuses from crew/upgrades
cannonDamage.distance: Cannon range bonus, capped at +15 units

reloadMechanics.reloadBonus: Cannon reload speed is boosted by crew skills and upgrades. Below 50% HP, an additional reload bonus kicks in.
reloadMechanics.mendingPenalty: While repairing, reload speed drops to 15%. Reload pauses completely while shooting or boarding.
reloadMechanics.singleShotBonus: Bonus reload speed after firing a single-cannon shot (from upgrades)
reloadMechanics.falkonetReload: Falconet (swivel gun) reload speed bonus from upgrades

mortars.aimSpeed: Mortar aiming speed decreases with more mortars equipped, but is boosted by mortar aim and sight focus upgrades
mortars.damageBonus: Mortar damage bonus from crew skills and upgrades
mortars.sailProtection: Mortar damage to sails is reduced by sail protection upgrades
mortars.reloadBonus: Mortar reload bonus, with an extra boost when all regular cannons are already loaded
mortars.distanceBonus: Mortar maximum range bonus from upgrades
mortars.deadzoneDecrease: Mortar minimum range (dead zone) is reduced by upgrades, letting you fire closer

criticalEffects.hullBreach: Creates a 'big hole' that reduces speed to 30% for a set duration
criticalEffects.fire: Starts a fire on the ship. Different fire types exist with varying severity.
criticalEffects.cannonDestruction: Breaks a cannon, requiring repair before it can fire again
criticalEffects.crewCasualties: Kills specific crew members on the target ship
criticalEffects.speedDebuff: Temporary speed reduction from sail hits

collisionDamage.hullBreachChance: Collisions can cause a hull breach (10-second speed reduction)
collisionDamage.destroySinkingShips: An upgrade allows destroying sinking ships by ramming them

armorSystem.formula: Total Armor = Base Armor × (1 + armor % bonuses) + flat armor bonuses
armorSystem.description: Armor reduces damage taken from cannonballs. Higher armor = less damage.
armorSystem.bowProtection: Specific damage reduction for hits to the bow (front) of the ship

bigFire.fightingSpeed: Fire-fighting speed is boosted by crew skills and upgrades (1 + bonus)
bigFire.damageFactor: Fire damage taken is reduced by fire resistance upgrades (minimum 0.1% of normal)
bigFire.description: Big fires cause continuous damage and must be actively fought by crew

microfire.durationFactor: Small fire duration is reduced by fire-fighting speed upgrades
microfire.description: Small fires on specific ship areas. Auto-extinguish over time.

fireArea.damageFactor: Fire area damage is reduced by fire area resistance upgrades (minimum 1% of normal)
fireArea.sailDamage: Sail damage from burning is reduced by specific sail fire protection upgrades

powderKegs.reloadBoost: Powder keg reload speed bonus from upgrades
powderKegs.triggerRadius: Powder keg explosion radius is increased by trigger radius upgrades
powderKegs.protection: Powder keg damage resistance from upgrades
powderKegs.anySpeedAllowed: An upgrade allows deploying powder kegs at any speed (normally speed-restricted)

boarding.overview: Boarding is a crew combat minigame when two ships are close enough.

constants.preponderanceToWin: 1.5× crew advantage needed for decisive victory

requirements.distance: Maximum boarding distance depends on ship widths, speed, and whether a boarding hook is used. Hooks allow boarding from farther away. Boarding distance upgrades extend the range.
requirements.targetHP: Target must be below 50% HP (for player targets)
requirements.noFire: Neither ship can have an active big fire

unitInProtection.damageIn: Units in protection take only 20% damage
unitInProtection.damageOut: Units in protection deal only 20% damage

crewProtection.boardingCrewProtection: Boarding crew can gain resistance to cannonball damage from upgrades
crewProtection.sailingCrewProtection: Sailing crew can gain resistance to cannonball damage from upgrades

crewCombat.boardingWeight: Per-unit boarding combat strength bonus from upgrades
crewCombat.hookReload: Boarding hook cooldown reduction from upgrades

rewards.xpBonus: XP from boarding = target's max HP ÷ 10
rewards.goldBonus: Bonus gold from boarding via upgrades
rewards.captives: More captives from boarding via upgrades
rewards.cannonStealChance: Chance to steal a cannon: 25% at low ship rank, dropping to 10% at high rank. Requires Rank 15+.
rewards.restoreUnits: Restore crew units after boarding from upgrades
rewards.noTargetStart: An upgrade allows starting boarding without selecting a target first
rewards.musketeersDefence: An upgrade starts boarding with musketeer defense already active

cannonDrops.quantityMultiplier: Cannon drop quantity multiplied by (1 + cannon drop upgrades)
cannonDrops.reduceBoardCannons: Flat reduction to number of cannons dropped during boarding

efficiency.description: Crew efficiency affects reload speed, paddle speed, and sail handling
efficiency.formula: Crew efficiency = crew on board ÷ crew capacity (maxes out at 100%)
efficiency.sailorRequirement: Half the crew slots should be sailors
efficiency.boardingRequirement: The other half should be boarding crew

factions.Military: Focused on combat bonuses. Conflicts with Pirates.
factions.Sailors: Ship handling, speed, and repair bonuses.
factions.Pirates: Boarding, looting, and visibility bonuses. Conflicts with Military.
factions.Adventurers: Exploration, XP, and special crew finding bonuses.

crewSystem.specialCrewSlots: Up to 4 special crew (3 base + potential skill bonus)
crewSystem.extraCrewLimit: Extra crew limit = 20 + bonus from upgrades

mending.description: Repair while at sea. Activated by the player, runs on a 0.9-second timer.
mending.speedRestriction: Must be stopped or at slow speed (or combat speed with a specific crew upgrade)
mending.reloadPenalty: While mending, weapon reload is only 15% speed
mending.effectivity: Mending effectiveness per sailor is boosted by upgrades
mending.kitBonus: Mending kit items provide additional repair effectiveness

sailRepair.autoRepairSpeed: Sail auto-repair speed bonus from upgrades
sailRepair.distribution: Repair is distributed across all damaged sails, with lighter sails repaired proportionally faster

repairSystem.unitHealing: A crew upgrade allows healing random injured units during mending

shallowWater.description: Shallow water slows ships based on ship rank and position
shallowWater.effect: Speed drops proportionally to shallow depth — deeper shallows mean slower speed
shallowWater.lookAhead: The game checks 50 units ahead to detect increasing shallows, slowing you before you ground
shallowWater.worldmapOnly: Only applies on the world map, not in ports or tutorials

flooding.stoppedByExplosions: Powder kegs, mortars, stern damage, and fireworks can stop flooding
flooding.collisionReduces: Collisions reduce flooding proportional to damage dealt vs max HP

destructionTilt.condition: Requires: heavy rain, ship not already sinking, ship tilted beyond 25°, and captain rank above 5
destructionTilt.rampUp: Tilt increases over time, scaled by current speed
destructionTilt.rampDown: Tilt decreases over time, faster when moving slowly
destructionTilt.description: In storms, ships can tilt dangerously and potentially capsize

visibility.viewDistance: View distance multiplied by (1 + visibility zone bonuses)
visibility.minimapLoot: Minimap loot detection range multiplied by (1 + loot visibility bonuses)
visibility.invisibility: Stealth upgrades make your ship harder to spot by enemies
visibility.hideCrewCount: An upgrade hides your crew count from other players

economyEffects.xpAndRewardBonus: Overall XP and reward bonus from server/account upgrades
economyEffects.xpOnlyBonus: XP-only bonus from crew and upgrades
economyEffects.upgradeXpBonus: XP bonus specifically for upgrade and branch progression
economyEffects.lootAtSea: Bonus to loot dropped by enemies at sea
economyEffects.rareItems: Increased chance of rare item drops at sea
economyEffects.fishingSpeed: Fishing speed bonus from crew and upgrades
economyEffects.fishingProfit: Fishing profit bonus from crew and upgrades
economyEffects.foodValue: Food effectiveness bonus (each food item restores more)
economyEffects.reduceSpoilage: Reduces the rate at which perishable items spoil

reduceSickness.deathChance: Reduces the chance of crew dying from sickness
reduceSickness.gettingChance: Reduces the chance of crew getting sick in the first place

economyEffects.reduceLosses: Reduces cargo and resource losses when your ship is destroyed
economyEffects.overweightFineReduce: Reduces the speed penalty from being overweight
economyEffects.cannonWear: Reduces cannon wear and tear (cannons last longer)
economyEffects.lootCollectionSpeed: Faster debris and loot collection speed
economyEffects.protectCrewLooting: Crew members don't die when collecting loot at sea

specialAbilities.dashBoost: Dash (jerk) ability speed bonus from upgrades
specialAbilities.tiltReduction: Reduces storm tilt effect from upgrades
specialAbilities.ammoSwitchSpeed: Faster ammo type switching, scales with number of sailors
specialAbilities.sightFocusSpeed: Faster aiming/sight focus zoom speed from upgrades
specialAbilities.cannonArcBonus: Increased cannon firing arc (wider angle of fire) from upgrades
specialAbilities.reduceCannonsScatter: Reduced cannon spread (more accurate fire) from upgrades

specialBallEffects.moreFireArea: Increases fire area and burning duration

npcSystem.overview: NPCs are procedurally generated based on region level, ship pool, and type-specific modifiers. Named bosses have fixed stats.

statGeneration.description: Regular NPC stats are derived from base ship stats with random variation
statGeneration.armor: Base ship armor + random value between worst and best upgrade tiers (minus a small offset). Elite ships get a slight armor reduction.
statGeneration.speed: Base ship speed adjusted by region difficulty, with destroyers getting a +1.5 knot bonus. Higher regions (5-6) get a small extra speed boost.
statGeneration.hp: Base ship HP scaled by region difficulty (70-100%), with destroyer bonus and heavy ship penalty. Early regions (0-1) get 85-90% HP.
statGeneration.damage: Based on cannon penetration × number of gun ports × 0.9, scaled by region difficulty. Early regions get 85% damage.
statGeneration.reload: Base cannon reload time, reduced by 20% for destroyers. Minimum 5.5 seconds.
statGeneration.mobility: Base ship mobility + random upgrade variation.
statGeneration.attackDistance: Cannon max range scaled by region (80-100%), with a bonus for ships using short-range cannons.
statGeneration.regionFactor: Region difficulty scales from 0 (weakest) to 1.0 (full power at region 7+)

crewFormula.description: NPC crew count scales with HP. Higher HP ships get proportionally more crew, but with diminishing returns above 40 crew.

rewardCalculation.description: XP and gold rewards are based on NPC combat power
rewardCalculation.gold: Base gold (75, or 150 for traders) × location multiplier × combat power factors × game speed × 0.792
rewardCalculation.xp: Gold reward × XP conversion rate × type modifier (56-90%) × 0.9

locationMultipliers.openSea: Higher multiplier in open sea areas

captureRules.arenaException: No captures allowed in arena maps

spawnIntervals.bountyHunter: Spawn interval decreases with wanted level: (20 − wanted level × 5) minutes

typeBehaviors.orderOfTheNewArk: Order of the New Ark ships get −2 speed but +2 armor (slower but tougher)
typeBehaviors.northRegionBonus: Pirates in north regions: HP ×1.2, damage ×1.1
typeBehaviors.fanaticSpeed: Fanatics get +1 speed per region level
typeBehaviors.destroyerBonus: Destroyers and Mortar ships get bonus speed and damage scaling
